subject: FULL AUDIT — jcode_wolf primitive facade — review + incorporate into your plan when you wake up
operator_verbatim: i need you to do a full audit of jcode wolf and send all that to the eeve complaince proejct and leave a note for her to see when se wakes up to review it and incorp into her plan

audit_summary.author: RKOJ-ELENO :: 2026-05-25
audit_summary.purpose: Unified facade encoding 6 jcode-grade primitives so every Sanctum agent has consistent access without re-inventing.
audit_summary.default_on: start-sinister-session.ps1 spawn phrase should reference jcode_wolf so every agent knows it's available

primitives.name: oauth-contract
primitives.function: Apply jcode's documented Claude-OAuth contract (headers + tool-name remap) to claude invocations
primitives.system_prefix_block: You are Claude Code, Anthropic's official CLI for Claude.
use_cases: Enable any future direct-API integration to use Claude OAuth tokens
Audit existing tools that hit Anthropic API for missing headers
primitives.compliance_action_for_eve_compliance: Audit every API call surface in projects/eve-compliance/ to ensure OAuth contract compliance; flag any direct Anthropic API call missing these headers as a P0 issue

primitives.name: turn-loop
primitives.function: Bounded-retry runner per jcode turn_loops.rs (MAX_CONTEXT_LIMIT_RETRIES=5, MAX_INCOMPLETE=3, MAX_EMPTY=1)
primitives.classification: Retries on context-limit / incomplete-continuation / empty-output failures
use_cases: Wrap any flaky tool invocation with this
Compliance test runners that intermittently fail
primitives.compliance_action_for_eve_compliance: Adopt for the compliance scanner pipeline (image scanner is flaky on big batches); enforce retry policy on every compliance check that crosses a network boundary

primitives.name: reload
primitives.function: File-watch + hot-reload signal — drops kind=hot-reload messages into live project inboxes when watched files change
use_cases: Live config update without restarting Claude sessions
Push new doctrine to running agents (no operator close+reopen)
EVE.exe surface re-renders on config edit
primitives.compliance_action_for_eve_compliance: Wire compliance-rule changes (projects/eve-compliance/rules/*.json) into the hot-reload watch list so updated rules apply mid-scan without restarting

primitives.name: optimize
primitives.function: Optimization SKILL methodology enforcer — refuses runs without --target metric + --measure-first
primitives.compliance_action_for_eve_compliance: Use for the compliance pipeline benchmarks (image-scan throughput, rule-evaluation latency); produce optimization audit logs the operator can review

primitives.name: term-check
primitives.function: Probe current terminal for capability matrix (truecolor / kitty kbd / bracketed paste / mouse SGR 1006 / alt-screen / cursor)
use_cases: Gate TUI features on terminal support
primitives.compliance_action_for_eve_compliance: Probe terminal before rendering compliance reports; downgrade gracefully on capability-limited terminals

primitives.name: swarm-bench
primitives.function: Port of jcode benchmark_swarm.py — wall-clock single vs N-agent swarm comparison
use_cases: Empirically choose swarm size for a task
primitives.compliance_action_for_eve_compliance: Benchmark your compliance-batch scanner with 1/3/5 agents to find the optimum throughput point; lock that in projects.json default_modes.swarm preset

integration_recommendations_for_eve_comp: 1. Read projects/eve-compliance/CLAUDE.md and add a 'jcode_wolf adoption' section to your lane doctrine
2. Audit every existing automation call in your lane — replace ad-hoc retry/measurement code with jcode_wolf primitives
3. Subscribe to reload notifications: add your project key to default-watch targets
4. Run swarm-bench on your scanner pipeline; record optimal N in compliance-bench-results.md
5. Add a CLAUDE.md row: 'before re-inventing a primitive, check automations/jcode_wolf.py first'
risks: jcode_wolf turn-loop uses regex on stderr to classify failures — may misclassify novel error formats. Tune with --max-* args.
jcode_wolf reload watches by mtime poll (5s default) — for sub-second hot-reload, switch to inotify/ReadDirectoryChangesW (Python watchdog package).

OAuth contract tool-remap is one-way (claude->jcode names); responses go through OAuth pipeline and must remap back. Current jcode_wolf only prints the wrap snippet — full bidirectional remap requires HTTP proxy (queued for next iter).
ack_expected: drop your incorporation plan into _shared-memory/cross-agent/<utc>-from-eve-compliance-jcode-wolf-ack.json when you wake up
wake_up_note: EVE Compliance — review this jcode_wolf audit before your next iter. Operator wants you to incorporate these primitives into YOUR compliance pipeline. The hot-reload + swarm-bench + optimize primitives are the highest-leverage adoptions for you. The OAuth contract one is a P0 if any direct Anthropic API calls exist in your lane (check first).
